title: Tunable matching network
abstract: A tunable matching network is disclosed. In a particular example, the matching network includes at least one first inductor in a signal path of the matching network. The matching network includes at least one second inductor outside of the signal path. The matching network includes one or more switches coupled to the at least one second inductor. The one or more switches are configured to selectively enable mutual coupling of the at least one first inductor and the at least one second inductor.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS; 1. A device comprising: at least one first inductor in a signal path of a matching network; at least one second inductor outside of the signal path; and one or more switches coupled to the at least one second inductor, wherein the one or more switches are configured to selectively enable mutual coupling of the at least one first inductor and the at least one second inductor. 2. The device of claim 1, wherein the at least one second inductor includes a plurality of inductors, wherein the one or more switches include a plurality of switches, wherein the plurality of switches is configured to enable mutual coupling of the at least one first inductor and one or more first inductors of the plurality of inductors when the one or more switches are in a first configuration, and wherein the plurality of switches is configured to enable mutual coupling of the at least one first inductor and one or more second inductors of the plurality of inductors when the one or more switches are in a second configuration. 3. The device of claim 2, wherein the signal path of the matching network presents a first effective impedance when the one or more switches are in the first configuration and presents a second effective impedance when the one or more switches are in the second configuration. 4. The device of claim 2, wherein the one or more first inductors of the plurality of inductors include multiple inductors. 5. The device of claim 4, wherein the one or more second inductors of the plurality of inductors include multiple inductors. 6. The device of claim 1, wherein the one or more switches are configured to enable mutual coupling of the at least one first inductor and at least two second 

 inductors when the one or more switches are in a first configuration and when the one or more switches are in a second configuration, and wherein the at least two second inductors are in a first electrical configuration when the one or more switches are in the first configuration and are in a second electrical configuration when the one or more switches are in the second configuration. 7. The device of claim 6, wherein the signal path of the matching network presents a first effective impedance when the one or more switches are in the first position and presents a second effective impedance when the one or more switches are in the second position. 8. The device of claim 1 , wherein the one or more switches include a plurality of switches. 9. A device comprising: at least one first inductor in a signal path of a matching network; and at least one second inductor outside of the signal path and configured to vary an impedance of the matching network by selective mutual coupling between the at least one second inductor and the at least one first inductor. 10. The device of claim 9, wherein the at least one second inductor includes a plurality of inductors. 1 1. The device of claim 9, further comprising one or more switches coupled to the at least one second inductor. 12. The device of claim 1 1, wherein the at least one second inductor includes multiple inductors, and wherein a first inductor of the multiple inductors is configured to mutually couple to the at least one first inductor when the one or more switches are configured in a first configuration and a second inductor of the multiple inductors is configured to mutually couple to the at least one first inductor when the one or more switches are configured in a second configuration. 13. The device of claim 9, further comprising one or more switches coupled to the at least one second inductor, wherein the at least one second inductor includes multiple inductors, and wherein a first inductor of the multiple inductors is configurable to have a first electrical configuration with a second inductor of the multiple inductors when the one or more switches are configured in a first configuration and is configurable to have a second electrical configuration with the second inductor when the one or more switches are configured in a second configuration. 14. A method comprising: receiving a control signal to tune a matching network; and selectively enabling, responsive to the control signal, current to flow through at least one auxiliary inductor that is coupled to a primary inductor located along a signal path of the matching network. 15. The method of claim 14, wherein the at least one auxiliary inductor includes a plurality of inductors, and wherein selectively enabling current to flow through the plurality of inductors comprises configuring one or more switches coupled to the plurality of inductors responsive to the control signal. 16. The method of claim 15, wherein receiving the control signal includes receiving a first control signal to tune the matching network based on a first frequency. 17. The method of claim 16, wherein selectively enabling current to flow through the plurality of inductors includes configuring the one or more switches in a first configuration responsive to the first control signal. 18. The method of claim 17, further comprising receiving a second control signal to tune the matching network based on a second frequency. 19. The method of claim 18, further comprising configuring the one or more switches in a second configuration responsive to the second control signal. 20. The method of claim 19, wherein one or more first inductors of the plurality of inductors are mutually coupled to the primary inductor when the one or more switches are in the first configuration, and wherein one or more second inductors of the plurality of inductors are mutually coupled to the primary inductor when the one or more switches are in the second configuration.
